name: M101 – Pinwheel Galaxy
description: A grand‑design spiral galaxy that shines at magnitude 7.9. Well‑placed high in the northern sky all night. Perfect for broadband RGB imaging.

name: M51 – Whirlpool Galaxy
description: An iconic interacting pair in Canes Venatici. The spiral structure is striking, and the bridge of stars between the two galaxies is a must‑capture.

name: M13 – Great Hercules Cluster
description: The brightest globular cluster in the north. Rising to a comfortable altitude by midnight, it will be a sparkling ball of stars through any telescope.

name: IC 1396 – Elephant’s Trunk Nebula
description: A vast emission nebula in Cepheus, loaded with dark lanes and bright rims. Narrowband filters (Ha, OIII) really make it pop.

name: NGC 6960 – Western Veil Nebula
description: A delicate supernova remnant that’s well‑placed for late‑night imaging. Its wispy filaments are a joy to capture with an OIII filter.

name: M57 – Ring Nebula
description: A small but brilliant planetary nebula in Lyra. High in the sky, it’s an easy target that shows its central star with enough exposure.

name: M27 – Dumbbell Nebula
description: Another bright planetary nebula, this time in Vulpecula. Its apple‑core shape is easily visible, and it takes colour beautifully.

name: NGC 7000 – North America Nebula
description: A huge emission region in Cygnus that’s best framed with a wide‑field telescope. Narrowband filters reveal incredible detail.
